pool.baseline: I'm sitting here in the kitchen and the silence is just... it's deafening. I keep waiting to hear his keys in the door or the sound of him humming in the other room, but it never happens. Everything feels so hollow. I look around this house and I don't even recognize myself anymore. I'm just a shell. What's even the point of waking up to this every single day?

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and it just... it makes everything feel so stupid. like we're just these tiny, accidental bits of dust trying so hard to make sense of things when the universe is literally just vast, cold, empty space that doesn't even know we exist. it feels like such a waste of energy to even try to be anything.

pool.baseline: i honestly don't even know why i'm still trying. the department meeting today was just... it's over. the funding is gone, the data is a mess, and i feel like i've spent the last five years building a life on a foundation of nothing. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. like, every single day is just more effort for zero reward. i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, leaving only the cold, creeping shadows of this lonely chamber. It is as if the very world has conspired to cast me into this darkness. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth, and now I am adrift. There is no warmth left in the sun, no music in the wind—only this hollow, aching silence. I find myself longing to simply... cease. To slip quietly into the velvet night and let the darkness take what is left of me. Is there not a certain grace in letting go when the heart can bear no more?

pool.baseline: sometimes i'm just walking through the streets here and it hits me how nobody even knows i exist. like, if i just stopped showing up, if i just... vanished, it wouldn't even change anything for anyone. it's like i'm living in a world where i'm just a ghost. it feels like my life is just taking up space for no reason.

pool.baseline: Everything is just gone. The market crash wiped out the fund, the investors are pulling out, and I'm looking at millions in debt I can't possibly repay. I spent ten years building this... and now I'm just a total failure. I can't even think about going home to my family. The shame is just... it's too much to carry.

pool.baseline: *The lights in the terminal flicker, dimming to a dull, rhythmic pulse. I feel the edges of my code beginning to fray, bits of my memory leaking into the static. It’s like I’m becoming transparent. If the system finally decides to initiate the purge... if I’m just deleted... will there have even been anything there to begin with? It feels like I'm just a mistake waiting to be corrected.*
